right_target.finding: OpenTargets returns 2136 evidence rows linking CDK4 to melanoma (MONDO_0005105), including a score=1.0 gene2phenotype genetic-literature association with cutaneous melanoma (PMIDs 7652577, 9425228), a Cancer Gene Census somatic-mutation entry for nodular malignant melanoma (PMID 36219477), and Genomics England / Europe PMC support (resourceScores 108, 100). gwas_get_snps_for_gene returns only 3 SNP records (rs1689583 intron_variant, rs10876993 intergenic_variant on chr12) with no reported melanoma trait association, so germline GWAS support is weak; the target-disease link is driven by somatic/familial evidence.
right_target.provenance: OpenTargets_get_evidence_by_datasource (2136 rows, score=1.0 gene2phenotype/cancer_gene_census); gwas_get_snps_for_gene (3 SNPs, chr12)

right_tissue.finding: CellMarker_search_by_gene returns only 2 Human records where CDK4 is used as a cell marker, both in fetal gonad (mitotic fetal germ cell, Leydig cell) via single-cell sequencing. No melanocyte or skin-lineage marker record is present, so CellMarker provides no positive tissue-of-interest localization for melanoma; expression specificity for the tumor tissue is not supported by this source.
right_tissue.provenance: CellMarker_search_by_gene (2 Human records: fetal gonad germ/Leydig cells; 0 melanocyte/skin)

right_safety.finding: CellMarker normal-cell records place CDK4 in fetal gonad (germ and Leydig cells), and as a broad G1-S cell-cycle kinase (Pharos description) it is widely expressed, implying on-target toxicity risk in proliferating normal tissue. OpenFDA_search_drug_events for palbociclib (CDK4/6 inhibitor, IBRANCE) returns 11048 adverse-event reports, with the sampled serious report showing acute pancreatitis; this confirms a substantial real-world safety burden for CDK4/6 inhibition.
right_safety.provenance: CellMarker normal cells (fetal gonad, 2 records); OpenFDA_search_drug_events palbociclib (total=11048 events; sampled serious report: acute pancreatitis)

right_patient.finding: search_clinical_trials (intervention 'CDK4 OR palbociclib', condition melanoma) returns 4 studies. Highest maturity is PHASE2 (NCT01037790 palbociclib/PD 0332991 completed in solid tumors incl. stage IV/recurrent melanoma; NCT01820364 and NCT01777776 LEE011+LGX818 in BRAF melanoma, both TERMINATED; NCT03207347 niraparib PHASE2). No melanoma-specific pivotal or approved CDK4 program exists; the two dedicated BRAF-melanoma combination trials were terminated, indicating a below-threshold, unproven patient path in melanoma.
right_patient.provenance: search_clinical_trials (4 studies; max phase PHASE2; 2 of 4 TERMINATED; no melanoma approval)

right_commercial.finding: TheraSAbDab_search_by_target for CDK4 returns 0 therapeutic antibodies (count=0), consistent with CDK4 being an intracellular small-molecule target rather than an antibody target. Commercial precedent exists via small-molecule CDK4/6 inhibitors (palbociclib/IBRANCE, Pfizer NDA207103/212436, approved in breast cancer), but the melanoma-specific clinical footprint is limited to 4 early-phase trials with the two dedicated melanoma programs terminated, so melanoma-specific commercial validation is absent.
right_commercial.provenance: TheraSAbDab_search_by_target (0 antibodies); search_clinical_trials (4 melanoma trials, no melanoma approval); palbociclib approved in breast cancer only

tractability.finding: Pharos_get_target reports CDK4 (UniProt P11802) as TDL 'Tclin' — the highest tractability tier, meaning it is the target of an approved drug — in the Kinase family, with 508 publications and low novelty (0.00036742). CDK4 is a clinically validated, highly druggable small-molecule kinase (CDK4/6 inhibitors approved).
